pairs.question: Who was Arnold I, Count of Loon, and what was his approximate lifespan?
pairs.answer: Arnold I (c. 1045–c. 1130) was the Count of Loon from around 1079. He was the son of Emmo, Count of Loon, and Suanhildis, daughter of Dirk III, Count of Holland, and his wife Othelandis.

pairs.question: What role did Arnold I play in resolving the conflict at Sint-Truiden Abbey in 1088?
pairs.answer: In 1088, Arnold I mediated a conflict at Sint-Truiden Abbey between Bishop Henry of Verdun and Emperor Henry IV, who had appointed rival abbots. His diplomacy led to the emperor transferring authority of the abbey to Arnold, effectively forcing Henry IV and his ally Godfrey of Bouillon to withdraw from the monastery.

pairs.question: How did Arnold I expand his domain beyond the County of Loon?
pairs.answer: Arnold I expanded his domain by marrying Agnes of Mainz, daughter of Gerhard I, Count of Rieneck. This union brought the County of Rieneck under his control.

pairs.question: Who were the known children of Arnold I, Count of Loon?
pairs.answer: Arnold I is believed to have had several children, including Arnold II (Count of Loon), Gertrude van Loon (1100–1154), Agnes van Loon (born c. 1100), and Beatrix van Loon (died after 1132). A son named Gerard mentioned in older publications is now widely regarded as non-existent due to a historical transcription error.

pairs.question: What evidence supports the claim that Arnold I was succeeded by his son Arnold II as Count of Loon?
pairs.answer: Historical records indicate that Arnold I died between 1125 and 1135. After 1125, a Count Arnold appears alongside his son also named Arnold, suggesting a generational transition. By 1135, a new Count Arnold with his own son and successor Louis is documented, implying the succession of Arnold II.

pairs.question: What was the marital connection between Gertrude van Loon and Conrad III of Germany?
pairs.answer: Gertrude van Loon (1100–1154), daughter of Arnold I, married Hugo XI, Count of Dagsburg. Their son, Hugo XII, married Luitgarde, who was the daughter of Berengar II of Sulzbach. Berengar’s son was Conrad III of Germany, making Gertrude the aunt of Conrad III.

pairs.question: How do historians distinguish between Arnold I and Arnold II, Count of Loon?
pairs.answer: Historians differentiate Arnold I and Arnold II by the timeline of their recorded activities. Arnold I was active until his death between 1125 and 1135, while Arnold II appears in records starting around 1125 as a co-count with his father and later as sole count with his own successor, Louis, by 1135.
